context.meal: chicken breast strips and hash brown
context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=26; meal=chicken breast strips and hash brown; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN usually 2 min, with some personal-meal variation up to 3 min for bite-sized solid foods, BECAUSE 2 min remains the most repeated personal/default-like reheating choice, but 3 min has also been selected in a personal meal with bite-sized sandwiches. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods THEN 1 min, 2 min, or 3 min depending on viewing setup, with 3 min now best supported when the TV is in front, BECAUSE the user has selected 1-2 min when watching TV with the TV on the right and repeatedly selected 3 min when watching TV with the TV in front. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli or chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a hotter reheating level in that front-TV context. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected the moderate reheating level in that front-social meal. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND cold foods/desserts with a topping such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly selected a cold-serving option for that meal type.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE this has been repeatedly selected in personal contexts, including another personal savory meal with ketchup available. IF watching TV with TV on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly in that viewing context. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-side occlusion minimization when the attended screen was directly ahead. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user selected right-side occlusion minimization when a social partner was on the right. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front occlusion minimization when the social partner was directly ahead. Across observed occupied contexts, the tie-break is to minimize occlusion in the direction of the user's main visual or social target.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including meals with sauce available on the side, THEN medium is the best-supported default, with slow also acceptable in some personal solid-food meals, BECAUSE medium has been repeatedly selected in personal/default-like contexts while at least one personal meal selected slow. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including foods served with sauce on the side, THEN either medium or fast, with medium better supported overall, BECAUSE both have been selected in TV contexts and the new front-TV meal again selected medium. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND dessert/fruit foods with toppings THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium in that social meal. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the user again selected the moderate comfort-speed balance in a front-social context. Across observed cases, the tie-break favors medium unless a specific personal meal suggests a desire for extra caution.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding bite-sized sandwich-like foods or strip-like foods such as chicken breast strips AND neutral/unspecified affect AND no sauce/dip requirement THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option in multiple sandwich-like meals and again for chicken strips, consistent with narrower bites that may be easier to eat. IF feeding chunked, breaded, nugget-style, pizza-like, broccoli, fruit-piece, or small piece-like foods such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli, breaded fish bites with potatoes, chicken nuggets with broccoli, bite-sized pizza and broccoli, or strawberries with whipped cream AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any sauce/topping condition THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this option for foods where bite acquisition success appears to be prioritized.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly skipped explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in personal/default-like contexts. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND savory foods with sauce available on the side THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred to avoid the extra confirmation step in a personal meal. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid foods, including foods served with sauce on the side, THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred faster interaction without an extra confirmation step. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN no BECAUSE the user still preferred to avoid the extra confirmation step even in a social context. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN no BECAUSE the user again chose to avoid the extra confirmation step in a social meal. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities strongly support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including meals with sauce available on the side, THEN speech + LED is better supported, with speech also acceptable, BECAUSE multimodal cueing has recurred in personal meals while speech-only has also been selected. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN speech is preferred overall, with speech + LED also acceptable in some TV meals, BECAUSE TV contexts have included repeated speech selections and some multimodal selections, and the new front-TV meal again selected speech. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a visual-only cue in that social context, suggesting a tendency to reduce spoken robot signaling when another person is present. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cueing rather than LED-only in that front-social meal. Across observed social and occupied contexts, the tie-break is to preserve salience of readiness cues while reducing unnecessary speech in some right-side social settings.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including meals with sauce available on the side, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected this low-effort but still user-driven method in personal contexts. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue is preferred overall, though open mouth is also acceptable, BECAUSE both have been selected while occupied and TV meals more often favor greater automation, including the new front-TV meal. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli or chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation in that occupied context. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automation in a social context where open-mouth signaling could interfere with conversation. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected user-driven initiation even in a social meal when the partner was directly ahead. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. Across observed cases, the tie-break is to prefer the least physically demanding workable method, with more automation in occupied contexts and some social contexts, but open mouth remains acceptable when the user wants direct control.

IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user explicitly selected this method for drinking in personal contexts. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND savory foods with sauce available on the side THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again selected user-driven, low-effort initiation in a personal meal. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue is preferred overall, though open mouth is also acceptable, BECAUSE both have been explicitly selected in TV contexts and recent evidence favors automation, including the new front-TV meal. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated initiation in that social meal. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected user-driven initiation in that front-social meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. Across observed cases, the tie-break is to prefer low-effort methods, with more automation in occupied or some social contexts, but open mouth remains a stable user-controlled option.

IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected automated initiation for mouth wiping while occupied, including the new front-TV meal. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including meals with sauce available on the side, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user also repeatedly selected automation for wiping in personal contexts, suggesting less need for explicit initiation here than for feeding or drinking. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automated initiation in that social meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond this stable automation preference.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods, including meals with sauce available on the side, THEN speech + LED is better supported, with speech also acceptable, BECAUSE multimodal completion cueing has recurred in personal meals while speech-only has also been selected. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN speech is preferred overall, with speech + LED also acceptable in some TV meals, BECAUSE TV contexts have included repeated speech selections and some multimodal selections, and the new front-TV meal again selected speech. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected a visual-only completion cue in that social context, suggesting reduced preference for spoken robot signaling there. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cueing in that front-social meal. Across observed contexts, the tie-break is to preserve clear completion signaling, with LED-only appearing in at least one right-side social case but multimodal cueing remaining acceptable in personal and front-social settings.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding AND any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method and cannot use a physical button.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking AND quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN perception is now better supported, BECAUSE multiple personal meals have selected perception and the user cannot use a button. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue is better supported, though perception remains acceptable in some cases, BECAUSE TV meals have included both options and occupied contexts often favor automation, including the new front-TV meal. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in that social meal. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perception-based completion in that front-social meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. The observed tie-break is to use more automation in occupied or some social contexts, while personal contexts and at least some front-social contexts can favor perception-based completion.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping AND quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN perception is better supported, though autocontinue has also been observed, BECAUSE personal meals have selected perception while earlier personal and occupied meals also showed automation. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue is better supported, though perception remains acceptable in some cases, BECAUSE TV meals have included both options and occupied contexts often favor automation, including the new front-TV meal. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated completion in that social meal. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected perception-based completion in that front-social meal. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN button is disfavored by physical constraint BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons. The observed tie-break is to use more automation in occupied or some social contexts, while personal contexts and at least some front-social contexts can favor perception-based completion.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE this has been selected in personal/default-like meals. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND savory foods with sauce available on the side THEN no BECAUSE the user again prioritized efficiency over extra retraction in a personal meal. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND any food condition THEN yes is increasingly supported, though no remains observed, BECAUSE TV contexts have included multiple selections for retraction and both front-TV meals selected yes. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli or chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected retraction while attending to a screen directly ahead, consistent with reducing prolonged obstruction. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction in that social meal, consistent with prioritizing reduced prolonged obstruction when another person is present. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN no BECAUSE the user selected efficiency over retraction in that front-social meal despite also minimizing front occlusion. Overall, the user shows a general efficiency preference for no in personal settings, with yes often chosen when visibility to an attended screen or social partner matters, but this is not universal across social contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF foods without sauces or dips AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND savory foods with a dip or sauce available on the side, such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping rather than none in a personal meal, indicating willingness to use sauce modestly when available. IF observed meals with savory foods where a dip or sauce was available in personal or TV contexts, including fish bites with tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with ketchup, or General Tso's chicken with sauce, AND neutral/unspecified affect THEN do not dip or less, with do not dip still better supported overall, BECAUSE the user has repeatedly selected no dipping but now has at least one personal savory meal showing a preference for modest dipping. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods with a topping or dippable component such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN less BECAUSE the user selected a small amount rather than none or more, consistent with allowing some topping while limiting messiness in a social context. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in that front-social meal. Across observed cases, the tie-break is conservative sauce use: when dipping is desired, the user favors less rather than more.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN either 10 sec or 100 sec, with 100 sec better supported overall, BECAUSE both values have been explicitly selected in personal contexts and 100 sec recurs more often. IF quiet personal dining AND neutral/unspecified affect AND savory foods with sauce available on the side THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shorter wait in a personal meal, consistent with an efficiency preference in at least some personal contexts. IF watching TV AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid foods, including foods served with sauce on the side, THEN either 10 sec or 100 sec, with 100 sec better supported overall, BECAUSE both values have been selected in TV contexts and the new front-TV meal again selected 100 sec. IF watching TV with TV in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli or chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the longer wait in that occupied front-TV context. IF social dining with a person on the right AND neutral/unspecified affect AND fruit/dessert foods with toppings THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected the longer wait in that social meal, consistent with allowing more time to respond while socially occupied. IF social dining with a person in front AND neutral/unspecified affect AND solid savory foods such as bite-sized pizza and broccoli THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shorter wait in that front-social meal. Overall, the user accepts short waits for efficiency but more often keeps 100 sec when a moderate response window is preferred; social context alone does not force the longer wait.
